---
title: 'catalog rm'
---

## Synopsis

```bash
xorq catalog [OPTIONS] remove [--sync/--no-sync] NAMES...
```

## Description

Remove one or more entries from the catalog by name. Removing an entry also removes all aliases that point to it.

The removal is committed atomically — the entry archive, metadata file, associated alias symlinks, and `catalog.yaml` are all updated in a single git commit.

## Options

`--sync / --no-sync`
: Synchronize with remotes before and after removal. Default: `--sync`.

## Related commands

`remove-alias`
: Remove one or more aliases without removing the underlying entry.

```bash
xorq catalog [OPTIONS] remove-alias [--sync/--no-sync] ALIASES...
```

## Examples

```bash
# Remove an entry (and its aliases)
xorq catalog --name my-catalog remove hash-abc123

# Remove multiple entries without syncing
xorq catalog --name my-catalog remove --no-sync hash-abc123 hash-def456

# Remove just an alias
xorq catalog --name my-catalog remove-alias my-alias
```

## See also

- [catalog add](add.qmd)
- [catalog list](ls.qmd)
